meta.title: AWS-DEA-5: Streaming, Lake Governance, and Analytics Operations

questions.stem: A data team needs ordered event ingestion with multiple independent consumers replaying the same stream. Which AWS service is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because `Amazon Kinesis Data Streams` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Amazon Kinesis Data Streams overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/streams/latest/dev/introduction.html)

questions.stem: A company wants a managed service that buffers streaming data, can invoke Lambda for transformation, and then lands the output in Amazon S3 or Amazon Redshift. Which service should it choose?
questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because `Amazon Data Firehose` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Amazon Data Firehose overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/firehose/latest/dev/what-is-this-service.html)

questions.stem: An engineer must replicate ongoing CDC changes from Amazon RDS into Amazon S3 with minimal custom code. Which service is designed for that?
questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because `AWS Database Migration Service` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[AWS DMS documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/dms/latest/userguide/Welcome.html)

questions.stem: A batch ingest process receives CSV files in Amazon S3 and needs to infer schema and register tables for analytics automatically. Which AWS Glue feature helps most?
questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because `Crawlers` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Defining crawlers in AWS Glue](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/add-crawler.html)

questions.stem: A producer writes records directly into an append-only stream. The consumers need near-real-time processing with low latency rather than delivery batching. Which choice is best?
questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because `Amazon Kinesis Data Streams` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Amazon Kinesis Data Streams overview](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/streams/latest/dev/introduction.html)

questions.stem: A team wants serverless ETL using Apache Spark to clean and join datasets stored in Amazon S3. Which service best matches?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `AWS Glue jobs` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[What is AWS Glue?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/what-is-glue.html)

questions.stem: A Glue ETL job reprocesses old input files every time it runs. Which feature should the engineer enable to process only new data when supported by the source?
questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because `Job bookmarks` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Tracking processed data using job bookmarks](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/glue/latest/dg/monitor-continuations.html)

questions.stem: A team needs SQL queries over files in Amazon S3 without provisioning infrastructure. Which service is the most direct choice?

options.text: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `Amazon Athena` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[What is Amazon Athena?](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/latest/ug/what-is.html)

questions.stem: A pipeline already lands transformed parquet files in Amazon S3. Analysts only need ad hoc SQL access. Which next step is simplest?

options.text: Create a crawler and query with Athena

questions.explanation:
**B** is correct because `Create a crawler and query with Athena` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

questions.stem: A streaming application needs custom stateful event processing logic over Kinesis records before writing results out. Which service is most appropriate?

options.text: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink

questions.stem: A team wants a petabyte-scale cloud data warehouse optimized for analytical queries on structured data. Which service should it use?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `Amazon Redshift` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Data distribution for query optimization](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/dg/t_Distributing_data.html)

questions.stem: In Amazon Redshift, which design choice helps colocate rows that are frequently joined on the same key across large tables?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `Distribution keys` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Data distribution for query optimization](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/dg/t_Distributing_data.html)

questions.stem: An architect wants Redshift table blocks stored in sorted order to improve range-restricted scans. Which table property is most relevant?

**A** is correct because `Sort key` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Sort keys](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/dg/t_Sorting_data.html)

questions.stem: A team has infrequently accessed raw objects that must remain immediately retrievable at low cost. Which Amazon S3 storage class is a common fit?
questions.explanation:
**C** is correct because `S3 Standard-IA` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Amazon S3 storage classes](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/storage-class-intro.html)

questions.stem: A Glue job needs permission to read from Amazon S3 and write to the Data Catalog. What should be attached to the job role?

questions.stem: A team wants an alert when a Kinesis stream falls behind because consumers are not keeping up. Which metric category should they monitor in CloudWatch?

options.text: Stream lag or iterator age related metrics

questions.stem: Which option best describes why teams often load curated dimensional data into Amazon Redshift instead of querying every workload directly in raw S3 files?

options.text: Redshift is built for analytical warehousing and optimized query performance
Redshift is the only AWS service that can store tabular data
Athena cannot read Glue Data Catalog tables

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `Redshift is built for analytical warehousing and optimized query performance` best fits the scenario based on the official documentation. The other options either solve a different problem, require more custom work, or do not provide the capability asked for.

[Data distribution for query optimization](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/dg/t_Distributing_data.html)
